name: Portuguese
register: Reverent contemporary Brazilian-Portuguese Bible register in the wording family of the João Ferreira de Almeida tradition — matching the Nova Almeida Atualizada (NAA, 2017) and Almeida Revista e Atualizada (ARA): dignified and warm, the cadence a believing reader trusts, but understandable to today's reader. Filial renderings lean on the beloved 'Pai Nosso' / 'Pai celestial' phrasing that tradition already gives to God-as-Father.
quote_convention: Brazilian editions use double quotation marks “ ” (aspas), with the travessão (em dash) —  introducing direct speech in narrative; European Portuguese editions use guillemets « ».
poetry_convention: Poetry (Psalms, Proverbs, canticles) set as indented verse lines, one poetic colon/stich per line, with a deeper secondary indent for the second half of a parallelism — the standard ARA/NAA layout.

glossary.note: Filial divine name; 'Pai celestial' is the exact wording the Almeida tradition already uses (e.g. Mt 6), so it reads as reverent, not novel. Never 'nosso Deus celestial'.
Primary filial divine name — a child addressing their own father. NEVER 'Deus' or 'Senhor' as a substitute.
Definite filial name; standard Almeida for 'the Father'. Not 'o Deus'.
Bare filial form (no possessive); matches 'Pai celestial' of the trusted text. Preferred over the Latinate 'Pai celeste' for warmth and familiarity.
Almeida retains the Aramaic 'Aba' followed by the Portuguese gloss 'Pai' (Rm 8:15; Gl 4:6; Mc 14:36), exactly as the tradition does — intimate childhood address.
NON-filial contrast term. Traditional word for the generic/absolute deity; use only where the English keeps 'God' as God-as-God, never for 'our Father'.
NON-filial contrast term. Almeida renders the covenant name YHWH as 'o SENHOR' in small capitals; distinct from the courtesy title 'o Senhor' (Lord) used of Jesus.
Fixed Almeida term; confirmed in preview (John 3).
Relational rendering, not by-identity — 'de nosso Pai', not 'de Deus' (cf. Mt 10:20 'Espírito de vosso Pai'). Carries the filial stem.
Standard Almeida Christological title; confirmed in preview (John 3).
The Son (Jesus) in relation to the Father; standard Almeida.
Fixed Christological title in the Almeida tradition — keep 'de Deus' here (title of identity), not 'do Pai'. Reviewer to confirm against 'Filho do Pai' preference.
'unigênito' is the beloved, deeply familiar Almeida rendering of John 3:16 (monogenēs). Modern alternative 'seu único Filho' (NAA) is acceptable but less resonant for most readers.
FHB renders 'kingdom of God' filially — 'de nosso Pai', not 'de Deus'. Carries the filial stem; confirmed in preview (John 3:3,5).
Standard; confirmed in preview.
Standard Almeida; confirmed in preview (John 3:28).
Standard Almeida transliteration (e.g. John 1:41; 4:25).
Standard Almeida term for berith/diathēkē — 'aliança', not the legal 'pacto'/'contrato'.

Renders Hebrew ḥesed (loyal covenant love). 'amor leal' captures the steadfast + covenant-loyal sense the way ESV 'steadfast love' does. Traditional Almeida alternatives: 'misericórdia' (ARA), 'benignidade' (ARC) — reviewer to confirm.
Renders raḥam / splanchnizomai (gut-level tender mercy); Almeida 'moveu-se de íntima compaixão' (Mt 9:36). 'compaixão' over 'misericórdia' to preserve the visceral tenderness.
Standard Almeida term for tsedeq/dikaiosynē.
Fixed Almeida phrase; confirmed in preview (John 3:16).
Almeida's wording for John 3:3,7 (gennēthē anōthen) — the phrase Portuguese Christians know by heart; confirmed in preview.
